factors.summary: Regulatory changes are impacting operations with the continued implementation of Positive Train Control.

evidence.text: The tunnel outage in late May and continued implementation and testing of Positive Train Control across a larger portion of our network also negatively impacted overall average train speed.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The continued implementation of Positive Train Control has negatively impacted train speeds, indicating operational challenges.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are stable with growth in volumes and ton-miles.

evidence.text: Gross ton-miles and revenue ton-miles increased 4% and 3% respectively compared to 2017, driven by a 3% increase in carloadings.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and revenue ton-miles have increased, reflecting stable and growing economic conditions.

factors.summary: Industrial and premium segments are growing fastest, while agricultural and energy segments face some challenges.

evidence.text: Industrial freight revenue increased due to volume growth, core pricing gains, and higher fuel surcharge revenue, partially offset by negative mix of traffic. Energy freight revenue increased due to mix of traffic and higher fuel surcharge revenue, but volume declined 1% in the second quarter compared to 2017 driven by a 10% decline in coal and coke shipments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Industrial and premium segments are growing, despite challenges in agricultural and energy sectors.

factors.summary: Labor costs are rising, driven by wage inflation and operational challenges.

evidence.text: Compensation and benefits expenses increased 3% in the second quarter and 2% in the year-to-date period compared to 2017 due to volume-related costs, network operational challenges including overtime expenses, increased Train, Engine and Yard (TE&Y) training expenses, and wage inflation.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising labor costs due to wage inflation and operational challenges are impacting the company's margins.

factors.summary: Positive volume growth and premium segment expansion are key catalysts for improved results.

evidence.text: Growth in shipments of international and domestic intermodal, frac sand, construction products, metals, petroleum products, industrial chemicals, plastics, and lumber more than offset declines in coal and agricultural products shipments. Freight revenue from premium shipments increased in the second quarter and year-to-date period compared to 2017 due to higher fuel surcharge revenue, volume growth, and core pricing gains, partially offset by negative mix of traffic.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive volume growth and premium segment expansion are driving improved results.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power, as evidenced by core pricing gains that helped mitigate cost challenges, particularly higher fuel prices. This pricing strength is further supported by higher fuel surcharge revenue, though there is a noted lag in fuel surcharge recovery due to fluctuating fuel prices.

evidence.text: Our second quarter operating ratio of 63.0% increased 1.1 points compared to 2017 mainly driven by more than one point of negative impact due to higher fuel prices, and other cost hurdles, which were partially mitigated by core pricing gains and volume growth leverage.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Higher fuel surcharge revenue resulted from higher year-over-year fuel prices, partially offset by a lag in fuel surcharge recovery due to the sequential increase in fuel price from the first quarter of 2018.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong pricing power and higher fuel surcharge revenue are mitigating cost challenges.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with evidence of constraint in certain operational areas and acceleration in specific industrial and premium segments. On one hand, network congestion and high freight car inventory levels have led to decreased train speeds, indicating operational challenges that may be limiting demand. On the other hand, growth in various shipment categories suggests that demand is accelerating in certain areas, particularly in industrial and premium segments.

evidence.text: Average train speed decreased 3% in both the second quarter and year-to-date periods of 2018 compared to 2017 largely due to network congestion on key routes and terminals created, in part, by high freight car inventory levels.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Growth in shipments of international and domestic intermodal, frac sand, construction products, metals, petroleum products, industrial chemicals, plastics, and lumber more than offset declines in coal and agricultural products shipments.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends are mixed, with operational constraints and growth in specific segments balancing each other.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting due to changes in commodity mix, which has caused variances in year-over-year increases between gross ton-miles, revenue ton-miles, and carloads. Additionally, the revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, particularly premium and intermodal, reflecting a strategic focus on improving profitability.

evidence.text: Changes in commodity mix drove the variances in year-over-year increases between gross ton-miles, revenue ton-miles and carloads.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Freight revenues increased 8% during the second quarter of 2018 compared to 2017, resulting from 4% volume growth, higher fuel surcharge revenue, and core pricing gains, partially offset by negative mix of traffic.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shifts towards higher-margin segments are improving revenue mix and profitability.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is evident, driven by volume increases and pricing gains, despite some challenges such as employee reductions and negative mix effects.

evidence.text: Employee levels were flat in the second quarter of 2018 compared to 2017 despite a 4% increase in volume levels.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Freight revenues increased 8% during the second quarter of 2018 compared to 2017, resulting from 4% volume growth, higher fuel surcharge revenue, and core pricing gains, partially offset by negative mix of traffic.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is evident with volume increases and pricing gains, indicating positive trends despite challenges.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly due to higher fuel prices, which have been partially mitigated by core pricing gains and volume growth leverage. The company is also offsetting these higher fuel costs through operational efficiency and pricing strategies.

evidence.text: Higher fuel prices... which were partially mitigated by core pricing gains and volume growth leverage.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Fuel costs were higher as gross ton-miles increased 4% compared to the same period in 2017 while the fuel consumption rate (c-rate) increased 6% compared to the second quarter of 2017.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs, particularly fuel prices, create a headwind despite mitigations, impacting margins negatively.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, with carloadings increasing by 4% in the second quarter and 3% year-to-date. Additionally, volume growth is evident across most segments, particularly in industrial and premium products, driven by factors such as increased construction activity, metals shipments, industrial chemicals, plastics, and lumber shipments.

evidence.text: Driven by a 4% increase in carloadings. Year-to-date, gross ton-miles and revenue ton-miles increased 4% and 3% respectively compared to 2017, driven by a 3% increase in carloadings.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Volume grew 6% and 4% in the second quarter and year-to-date period, respectively, compared to 2017 driven by growth in construction products due to increased activity in the South, increased metals shipments due to strength in domestic energy markets, higher industrial chemicals and plastics, and growth in lumber shipments due to a stronger housing market.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong volume growth across segments driven by increased activity and demand, indicating significant positive developments.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to higher fuel and labor costs, despite revenue growth. The operating ratio increased, driven by negative impacts from higher fuel prices, network operational challenges, and other cost hurdles.

evidence.text: Our second quarter operating ratio of 63.0% increased 1.1 points compared to 2017 mainly driven by more than one point of negative impact due to higher fuel prices, and other cost hurdles...
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Operating expenses increased $321 million and $513 million in the second quarter and year-to-date periods, respectively, compared to 2017 driven by higher fuel prices, volume-related costs, network operational challenges, depreciation, increased state and local taxes and inflation.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to higher costs, indicating a negative trend despite revenue growth.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is mixed, with some improvements in average terminal dwell time but overall productivity is declining due to increased terminal dwell times and network congestion.

evidence.text: Average terminal dwell time increased 4% and 6% in the second quarter and six-month period, respectively, compared to 2017 largely due to network congestion on key routes and terminals...
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Although we realized sequential improvement in our average terminal dwell time in the second quarter of 2018 compared to the first quarter of 2018, both our average train speed and average terminal dwell in the second quarter of 2018 still lagged when compared to 2017.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining productivity due to congestion and dwell times, despite some improvements, shows a negative trend.

factors.summary: Management confidence is evident, with significant share buybacks and capital investments indicating confidence in the company's future. However, management also appears cautious, focusing on operational improvements and cost management to handle challenges while maintaining productivity.

evidence.text: Cash used in financing activities increased $193 million in the first six months of 2018... driven by a $5.1 billion net increase in additional debt, which was offset by a $5.1 billion increase in shares repurchased...

Despite these challenges, we handled volume growth of 4% with workforce levels flat compared to the second quarter of 2017, demonstrating progress in other resource productivity initiatives, including engineering capital projects and management and administrative reductions.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals from management with significant investments but cautious operational focus, leading to a neutral assessment.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include network congestion and high freight car inventory levels, which have negatively impacted average train speed and terminal dwell. Additionally, operational challenges such as tunnel outages and the implementation of Positive Train Control, along with rising fuel costs and inflationary pressures, further contribute to these risks.

evidence.text: Average train speed decreased 3% in both the second quarter and year-to-date periods of 2018 compared to 2017 largely due to network congestion on key routes and terminals created, in part, by high freight car inventory levels.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Network congestion on key routes and in terminals created, in part, by high freight car inventory levels negatively impacted overall fluidity, average train speed, and terminal dwell. The tunnel outage in late May and continued implementation and testing of Positive Train Control across a larger portion of our network also negatively impacted overall average train speed.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Multiple near-term risks like congestion and operational challenges significantly impact performance, indicating very negative sentiment.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategic focus on infrastructure investment and operational efficiency, supported by a $3.3 billion capital plan. However, the company also faces challenges related to inflationary cost pressures on transportation-related contract services, which may impact its ability to achieve sufficient returns on these investments.

evidence.text: We expect our 2018 capital plan to be approximately $3.3 billion, which may be revised if business conditions or the regulatory environment affect our ability to generate sufficient returns on these investments.
We also incurred inflationary cost pressures on transportation-related contract services incurred at our subsidiaries that broker intermodal and transload services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear long-term strategy with significant capital investment, despite inflationary pressures, shows positive developments.

factors.summary: Fuel prices and network performance are key sensitivities impacting the company's results. Higher fuel prices have been partially mitigated by core pricing gains and volume growth, but network congestion and outages have negatively impacted train speeds and overall performance.

evidence.text: Higher fuel prices... which were partially mitigated by core pricing gains and volume growth leverage. The tunnel outage in late May and continued implementation and testing of Positive Train Control across a larger portion of our network also negatively impacted overall average train speed.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Higher fuel surcharge revenue resulted from higher year-over-year fuel prices, partially offset by a lag in fuel surcharge recovery due to the sequential increase in fuel price from the first quarter of 2018. Network congestion on key routes and in terminals created, in part, by high freight car inventory levels negatively impacted overall fluidity, average train speed, and terminal dwell.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher fuel prices and network congestion have negatively impacted performance, despite some mitigations.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is negatively impacted by network congestion, high inventory levels, and operational challenges such as tunnel outages and Positive Train Control implementation.

evidence.text: Network congestion on key routes and terminals created, in part, by high freight car inventory levels.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Network congestion on key routes and in terminals created, in part, by high freight car inventory levels negatively impacted overall fluidity, average train speed, and terminal dwell. The tunnel outage in late May and continued implementation and testing of Positive Train Control across a larger portion of our network also negatively impacted overall average train speed.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Network congestion and operational challenges significantly impair supply chain efficiency.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are elevated, primarily due to high freight car inventory, which has led to network congestion on key routes and terminals. This congestion has negatively impacted overall fluidity, average train speed, and terminal dwell times.

evidence.text: High freight car inventory levels created network congestion on key routes and terminals.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Network congestion on key routes and in terminals created, in part, by high freight car inventory levels negatively impacted overall fluidity, average train speed, and terminal dwell.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: High inventory levels cause network congestion, negatively affecting fluidity and performance.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing due to investments in locomotives and freight cars, but operational performance remains mixed with some improvements and lingering challenges.

evidence.text: Locomotives and freight cars include lease buyouts of $210 million in 2018 and $144 million in 2017.

Although we realized sequential improvement in our average terminal dwell time in the second quarter of 2018 compared to the first quarter of 2018, both our average train speed and average terminal dwell in the second quarter of 2018 still lagged when compared to 2017.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Capacity utilization is mixed with some improvements but lingering challenges.

factors.summary: The company is undergoing headcount restructuring, with a 1% reduction in employees year-to-date. This restructuring is part of a broader contraction mode, including layoffs and workforce reductions.

evidence.text: Employee levels decreased 1% compared to 2017 as volumes grew 3%
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

evidence.text: Approximately 250 employees across all crafts were furloughed, and approximately 200 high-horsepower, road locomotives were in storage.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Headcount restructuring, including layoffs, indicates workforce contraction.

factors.summary: Labor relations appear to be mixed, with some strain indicated by ongoing negotiations and arbitration, despite evidence of stability in other areas.

evidence.text: A coalition of two unions (representing about 20% of our agreement work force) agreed to the wage pattern contained in the tentative agreement and pursued final and binding arbitration on the health plan provisions. An arbitration award was issued in UPRR's favor in May.

Lower personal injury expense due to our continued improvement in employee safety performance, reduced costs for destroyed equipment owned by third parties, and lower freight damage expense partially offset these increases compared to 2017.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Labor relations show mixed signals with some strain and areas of stability.
